title: Computer Architecture: Programmer and Implementer Visions
summary: Distinguishes ISA from machine organisation, contrasts user/programmer/implementer views, and explains the language translation hierarchy.
content_points: Implementer's view: detailed design down to gate level (ALU, control unit, registers, datapath).
Language hierarchy: machine language → assembly language → high-level languages.
Assembler: assembly → machine code. Compiler: high-level → machine (directly or via assembly).
Advantages of assembly: efficiency, hardware access. Advantages of high-level: portability, productivity.
learning_objectives: Describe the role of assembler and compiler
Compare the advantages of assembly and high-level languages
